description: Official KVQuant (Int4 K + Int4 V, NUQ per-channel) vs CDA (K4V2+HMMA) decode-attention latency.
kvquant_shape: H_q=32 H_kv=32 D=128 (Llama-2-7B MHA, B=1 hardcoded). Int4 NUQ K + Int4 NUQ V with per-channel codebook (K) / per-token codebook (V). RoPE fused in K-matmul.
cda_shape: H_q=32 H_kv=8 D=128 (Llama-3.1-8B GQA, group_size=4). K4V2 codebook + fp32 norms.

kv_memory_per_token.kvquant_int4_int4_mha_32heads: 32 heads * 128 D * (4b K + 4b V) + per-channel K LUT overhead + per-token V LUT overhead ≈ 1024 B/token plus LUT fetch
kv_memory_per_token.cda_k4v2_gqa_8heads: 8 heads * (4b K + 2b V + 8B norms) per slot = 104 B/token total
kv_memory_per_token.ratio: CDA ≈ 9.85x less KV memory per token

caveats: KVQuant's released CUDA kernel is MHA-only AND B=1 hardcoded (kcache/vcache have no batch dim).
Non-sparse (dense) KVQuant path used here; their full method also adds 1% sparse outlier tracking which would cost extra.
Modern GQA models (Llama-3/Qwen/Mistral) cannot be served by KVQuant's current kernel without re-engineering.
Both paths use fp16 softmax numerics.

N_4K.note: CDA ≈ KVQuant at short N; QuaRot 3.76× slower

summary.headline: CDA 1.00-6.24× faster than KVQuant's official kernel at B=1, with CDA's advantage growing linearly in N (expected — 9.85× HBM saving).
summary.kvquant_vs_quarot: KVQuant kernel is 1.39-3.76× faster than QuaRot's across the same N range (better NUQ+RoPE fusion).
summary.key_narrative: Both KVQuant and QuaRot follow dequantize-then-attend. Even the faster KVQuant loses to CDA at N≥16K because CDA computes attention on 4-bit K + 2-bit V indices directly — no FP16 K/V materialization ever.
